host: Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX (Arrow Lake-HX, 24 cores; AVX2 + AVX-VNNI + F16C, no AVX-512)
harness: verify/cpu_simd_bench.c (links the qjl-cpu / polarquant-cpu plugin static libs; times the dispatched SIMD entrypoints)
note: The thread-sweep rows below were captured while two sibling agents were each running a -j24 fork CUDA build (load avg ~95) — they are pessimistic and the thread scaling is dominated by contention, not the kernels. The reliable low-load figures: qjl_score_fp32 ~23 ns/out before this pass (recorded 3005.4 us / 131072 in cpu_avxvnni_2026-05-11.json) -> ~9 ns/out after the partial-sum-LUT + 4x VGATHERDPS rewrite (~2.5x at this 131072-output working set; ~8x on the L2-resident 32768-output qjl_bench --throughput workload: 122.9 -> ~15 ns). qjl_score_i8 (AVX-VNNI, unchanged this pass) ~4-5 ns/out. polar_preht_dot (AVX2, optimized last pass) ~22 ns/out and scales ~7-8x at 16 threads on a quiet box. parity: avx2 score 256/256 worst rel 2.5e-6 vs the qjl fixture; not bit-identical to qjl_score_qk_ref (FP reassociation, same as the prior AVX2 path).
